Discovery: The US Army consolidated 75 active contracts into a single 10-year $10B Enterprise Agreement with Palantir (announced Apr 27 2026), while the Pentagon raised the Maven Smart System AI contract ceiling to $1.3B (up from $480M); this structural shift from project-based to infrastructure-tier revenue coincides with DoD committing $32B in FY2026 H1 alone to AI, cybersecurity, and data analytics — making Palantir the de facto AI operating system for US military and intelligence.
growth_picks.analyst_take: US Army $10B 10-year EA; Maven ceiling $1.3B; Karp 1.47M shares bought — thesis real but Fwd PE 75.7 fails value gate; wait for $100-115

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Q2 FY2026 blowout: $4.01B revenue, $3.16 EPS, $342M FCF; $600M buyback; BUT insiders net sold $5M over 3 months (6 sellers vs 1 buyer); stock at ~$200 above analyst consensus $135-145 (Tier 1: Q2 earnings; Tier 1: Form 4 net selling)

Discovery: On April 27, 2026 Sanmina announced a $600M open-ended stock buyback (no expiration) immediately after Q2 FY2026 earnings smashed estimates on AI infrastructure demand — the company had already exhausted its prior buyback program ($160M repurchased in Q2 alone) — stock surged 21% on April 28; caution: insiders are net sellers ($5M net over 3 months, 6 sellers vs 1 buyer), suggesting buyback is capital return, not insider conviction at these prices.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Post-21% gap stock above analyst targets $135-145; insider net selling $5M; wait for consolidation toward $190-200

catalysts_general: Reports of Amazon Trainium 3/4 and Microsoft Maia shifting to Broadcom from Marvell; analyst median target $115 vs current $153; potential ASIC design win loss not yet priced in (Tier 3: Bernstein semiconductor note; Tier 5: Seeking Alpha)

Discovery: 18 hyperscaler custom ASIC design wins (AWS + Microsoft) with 20% custom ASIC revenue growth guided for FY2027; February 2026 acquisition of Celestial AI adds photonic interconnect capability targeting $1B annualized run rate by FY2029.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AWS Trainium 3/4 and Microsoft Maia design wins reportedly shifted to Broadcom; stock at $153 above analyst median $115; thesis compromised

Near-zero revenue, micro-cap photonics speculation — no commercial-stage business
